regions.text: Here are the twenty-six letters used in English with the English names for them spelled out after them: a (ay), b (bee), c (see), d (dee), e (ee), f (eff), g (jee), h (aich), i (eye), j (jay), k (kay), l (ell), m (emm), n (enn), o (oh), p (pee), q (kyou), r (are), s (ess), t (tee), u (you), v (vee), w (double-U), x (eks), y (wy), z (zee/zed).
Some letters represent multiple sounds. For example, 'a' sounds different in bag, part, again, say, was, any, and fall.
The letter 'e' varies in bell, week, older, earth, or hear, and can be silent. Letters like 'a' and 'g' sound different in bag versus age.
Reading English is easier than Chinese but harder than purely phonetic languages. The book 'First Steps in Reading English' is recommended for beginners.
